questions: You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. You accidentally say something insensitive at a dinner party and everyone goes quiet. How do you feel afterward?

You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. You fail a professional certification exam that most of your colleagues passed on the first try. How do you react?

You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. You realize you've been pronouncing a common word wrong your entire life when someone corrects you in front of a group. What happens inside you?

You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. You overshare something personal in a meeting and immediately see the discomfort on people's faces. How do you feel?

You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. Your parent compares you unfavorably to your sibling's achievements during a family gathering. How do you respond internally?

You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. You're caught not knowing something basic in your own field during a job interview. What do you feel?
You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. You find out a group of friends had a gathering without inviting you. How do you process it?

You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. You lose your temper at your child over something trivial and see the hurt in their eyes. How do you feel about yourself?

You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. An old friend reminds you of something embarrassing you did years ago in front of new people. What's your reaction?
You're someone with solid self-regard who doesn't question their own worth, even when things go wrong. You show up to a formal event significantly underdressed and everyone notices. How do you feel?
